root_text.an3.92:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, kassako gahapati sīghaṁ sīghaṁ khettaṁ sukaṭṭhaṁ karoti sumatikataṁ.
root_text.an3.92:1.4: Sīghaṁ sīghaṁ khettaṁ sukaṭṭhaṁ karitvā sumatikataṁ sīghaṁ sīghaṁ bījāni patiṭṭhāpeti.
root_text.an3.92:1.5: Sīghaṁ sīghaṁ bījāni patiṭṭhāpetvā sīghaṁ sīghaṁ udakaṁ abhinetipi apanetipi.
root_text.an3.92:1.6: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, tīṇi kassakassa gahapatissa accāyikāni karaṇīyāni.
root_text.an3.92:1.7: Tassa kho taṁ, bhikkhave, kassakassa gahapatissa natthi sā iddhi vā ānubhāvo vā:
root_text.an3.92:1.8: ‘ajjeva me dhaññāni jāyantu, sveva gabbhīni hontu, uttarasveva paccantū’ti.
root_text.an3.92:1.9: Atha kho, bhikkhave, hoti so samayo yaṁ tassa kassakassa gahapatissa tāni dhaññāni utupariṇāmīni jāyantipi gabbhīnipi honti paccantipi.
root_text.an3.92:2.1: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, tīṇimāni bhikkhussa accāyikāni karaṇīyāni.
root_text.an3.92:2.4: imāni kho, bhikkhave, tīṇi bhikkhussa accāyikāni karaṇīyāni.
root_text.an3.92:2.5: Tassa kho taṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno natthi sā iddhi vā ānubhāvo vā:
root_text.an3.92:2.6: ‘ajjeva me anupādāya āsavehi cittaṁ vimuccatu sve vā uttarasve vā’ti.
root_text.an3.92:2.7: Atha kho, bhikkhave, hoti so samayo yaṁ tassa bhikkhuno adhisīlampi sikkhato adhicittampi sikkhato adhipaññampi sikkhato anupādāya āsavehi cittaṁ vimuccati.
root_text.an3.92:3.2: ‘tibbo no chando bhavissati adhisīlasikkhāsamādāne, tibbo chando bhavissati adhicittasikkhāsamādāne, tibbo chando bhavissati adhipaññāsikkhāsamādāne’ti.

translation_text.an3.92:1.1: “Mendicants, a farmer has three urgent duties.
translation_text.an3.92:1.3: A farmer swiftly makes sure the field is well ploughed and tilled.
translation_text.an3.92:1.4: Next they swiftly plant seeds in season.
translation_text.an3.92:1.5: When the time is right, they swiftly irrigate or drain the field.
translation_text.an3.92:1.6: These are the three urgent duties of a farmer.
translation_text.an3.92:1.7: That farmer has no special power or ability to say:
translation_text.an3.92:1.8: ‘Let the crops germinate today! Let them flower tomorrow! Let them ripen the day after!’
translation_text.an3.92:1.9: But there comes a time when that farmer’s crops germinate, flower, and ripen as the seasons change.
translation_text.an3.92:2.1: In the same way, a mendicant has three urgent duties.
translation_text.an3.92:2.3: Undertaking the training in the higher ethics, the higher mind, and the higher wisdom.
translation_text.an3.92:2.4: These are the three urgent duties of a mendicant.
translation_text.an3.92:2.5: That mendicant has no special power or ability to say:
translation_text.an3.92:2.6: ‘Let my mind be freed from defilements by not grasping today! Or tomorrow! Or the day after!’
translation_text.an3.92:2.7: But there comes a time—as that mendicant trains in the higher ethics, the higher mind, and the higher wisdom—that their mind is freed from defilements by not grasping.
translation_text.an3.92:3.1: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an3.92:3.2: ‘We will have keen enthusiasm for undertaking the training in the higher ethics, the higher mind, and the higher wisdom.’
